name: CLI binary
purpose: The rcf command-line entry point. Map verbs to store, query, view or build operations and render results and errors for a terminal.
responsibilities: Parse command-line arguments and route each verb to the matching core operation
Render results as readable terminal output by default
Honour quiet and verbose flags for scripting and diagnostics
Surface structured errors as human-readable messages with the underlying detail available
internalStructure: A small command router maps each verb (init, view, read, create, update, delete, coverage, trace, impact, build, validate) to a function in the store, query, view or build adapter. A formatter renders results for a terminal.

interfaces.name: init
interfaces.description: Initialise a new RCF project on disk.

interfaces.name: view
interfaces.description: Generate the diagram and the static page.

interfaces.name: create
interfaces.description: Create a document with parent linkage.

interfaces.name: delete
interfaces.description: Delete a document with reference safety.

interfaces.name: coverage
interfaces.description: Report coverage gaps across the tree.

interfaces.name: trace
interfaces.description: Trace forward or back along the intent chain.

interfaces.name: impact
interfaces.description: Report what is affected by a document change.

interfaces.name: build
interfaces.description: Assemble a build prompt or mark a slice done.

interfaces.name: validate
interfaces.description: Validate every document and list broken references.

dependencies.name: Document store
dependencies.description: All filesystem access goes through the store.

dependencies.name: View renderer
dependencies.description: The view verb routes to the renderer.

dependencies.name: Query engine
dependencies.description: The coverage, trace and impact verbs route to the query engine.

dependencies.name: Build adapter
dependencies.description: The build verbs route to the build adapter.

tradeoffs: A single binary keeps the install and discovery story simple; a fragmented set of binaries would be more orthogonal but is overkill for a single project.
notes: Verbs are kept close to the spoken vocabulary of the method so a non-coder can guess the command from the document type.
